channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines

item.title: How David Cross Gets Ready for a Night of ‘Dangerous’ Comedy
item.description: Mr. Cross, known for “Arrested Development” and “Mr. Show,” isn’t afraid to say what should not be said.
item.dc:creator: John Leland
item.category: Mr Show with Bob and David (TV Program)

media:content.media:credit: Ye Fan for The New York Times

item.title: How David Cross Gets Ready for a Night of ‘Dangerous’ Comedy
item.description: Mr. Cross, known for “Arrested Development” and “Mr. Show,” isn’t afraid to say what should not be said.
item.dc:creator: John Leland and Ye Fan

media:content.media:credit: Ye Fan for The New York Times

item.title: Reseña de ‘Hexes of the Deadwood Forest’ de Agnieszka Szpila
item.description: El superventas polaco “Hexes of the Deadwood Forest” es como un sueño febril posporno de realismo mágico de Europa del Este mezclado con “El placer de amar” pero con plantas.
item.dc:creator: Sadie Stein

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for April 11, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: In a Restaurant’s Name, a Message of Grief and Hope for Palestinians
item.description: Hinds Hall near Columbia University is a place to find Palestinian cuisine and to remember a 5-year-old killed in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Sharon Otterman

media:content.media:credit: Hiroko Masuike/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Hinds Hal is a nod to Columbia’s pro-Palestinian demonstrators.

item.title: Mamdani, Leaning Into ‘Sewer Socialism,’ Gets His Hands Dirty
item.description: The mayor has embraced the decades-old concept of “sewer socialism,” hoping to improve New Yorkers’ lives by focusing on public services.
item.dc:creator: Emma Goldberg

media:content.media:credit: Juan Arredondo for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani helped the Sanitation Department pick up trash in the Bronx on Thursday.

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Vance Lands in Pakistan for Peace Talks With Iran
item.description: There was uncertainty about whether a cease-fire would hold and the two sides could reach a long-term deal. Vice President JD Vance struck an optimistic but cautious tone before he left for the talks.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Pool photo by Jacquelyn Martin

item.title: Iran Looks to Project Unity With Large Delegation to Peace Talks
item.description: At least 70 people are in the team that is scheduled to negotiate with the American side in Pakistan on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Pakistan's Ministry of Foreign Affairs, via Reuters
media:content.media:description: A photograph released by Pakistan’s government showing the Iranian delegation led by the speaker of Iran’s parliament, Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, second from right; and Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi, second from left. They were welcomed by Pakistan’s army chief, Asim Munir, left; and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar, right, in Islamabad, Pakistan, on Friday.

item.title: Los líderes de Irán consideran que su posición en la guerra con EE. UU. ha mejorado
item.description: Para los dirigentes teocráticos iraníes, sobrevivir al ataque estadounidense-israelí significa una victoria. Pero es posible que su próxima crisis ya se esté gestando.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati and Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Una mujer ondea una bandera iraní en Teherán. Los dirigentes iraníes están entablando nuevas negociaciones tras el alto el fuego con una nueva serie de exigencias.

item.title: Taller intensivo de cine con Warner Herzog: 11 días, 10,000 dólares y caos
item.description: En las Azores, 50 artistas rodaron cortos con Werner Herzog en un curso intensivo de 11 días; una oportunidad única que implicaba pagar miles de dólares y adaptarse a lo imposible.
item.dc:creator: Saskia Solomon and Erin Schaff

media:content.media:credit: Erin Schaff/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Jordan McAfee-Hahn, left, shows Mr. Herzog and his longtime cinematographer, Peter Zeitlinger, footage from “Lola Dreams of the Sea,” a work in progress featuring a cow drawn to the ocean.

item.title: El caso Minerliz: 20 años sin respuestas
item.description: El crimen de una adolescente en el Bronx quedó sin resolver durante dos décadas, hasta que una maniobra ingeniosa de detectives produjo una pista decisiva.
item.dc:creator: Michael Wilson

media:content.media:credit: Elias Williams para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: El detective James Menton, conocido por su ingenio para obtener muestras abandonadas de ADN, en el New Rochelle Diner.

item.title: Growing List of Orban Loyalists Defecting Before Critical Election
item.description: With Prime Minister Viktor Orban seeming vulnerable before Sunday’s vote, criticism is growing from within institutions his party once counted on for support.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Higgins and Lili Rutai

media:content.media:credit: Akos Stiller for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Zalan Alkonyi in Budapest, Hungary’s capital. As a researcher focused on Russia, he says he has come under pressure to toe the government line.

item.title: Iran Has Been Consistent in War. Will It Be Consistent in Peace Talks?
item.description: While President Trump’s war aims have changed by the moment, Iran has stuck to firm demands. The question is whether it will compromise in peace negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Neil MacFarquhar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Pro-government demonstrators burning the American and Israeli flags on Wednesday in Tehran after a cease-fire was announced.

item.title: How Lu Xun, a Famous Chinese Writer, Became a Cute Communist Mascot
item.description: The Chinese Communist Party has turned Lu Xun, a Mao-era hero who excoriated the establishment, into a bland, Disney-style character.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Higgins and Qilai Shen

media:content.media:credit: Qilai Shen for The New York Times

item.title: In New War With Israel, Hezbollah Defies Notion That It Was Crippled
item.description: The Iran-backed Lebanese militant group surprised many with the intensity of its attacks on Israel in the current conflict.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: An Israeli soldier at the site of a Hezbollah missile strike in Haniel, Israel, last month.

item.title: Fallout of War Piles Economic Pain Onto Europe’s Political Stress
item.description: Europe is finding itself on the outs with Russia, China and the U.S., in what’s amounting to its very own “Mean Girls” moment.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Cohen
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Patrick Junker for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A liquefied natural gas terminal in Germany. Most of that L.N.G. comes from the United States, underscoring Europe’s vulnerability to American supplies.

item.title: Corrections: April 11, 2026
item.description: The following Editors’ Note and corrections appeared in print on Saturday, April 11, 2026.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Generator Of Economy Is Feeling The Pressure
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Saturday, April 11, 2026.

item.title: Artemis Astronauts Splash Down After Historic Lunar Flyby
item.description: The four astronauts aboard Artemis II splashed down at 8:07 p.m. Eastern time in the Pacific Ocean near San Diego on Friday, concluding their historic 10-day mission, the first to send humans to the moon in more than 50 years.
item.dc:creator: Jackeline Luna

media:content.media:credit: NASA, via Reuters

item.title: Gabbed, Gabbed, Gabbed
item.description: Kareem Ayas charms solvers with a chatty themeless puzzle.
item.dc:creator: Caitlin Lovinger

media:content.media:credit: Patrick Seeger/DPA, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: I’ve got two birds on the line for you.

item.title: La misión de Artemis II de la NASA regresa a la Tierra con éxito
item.description: La tripulación de la NASA amerizó en el océano Pacífico, cerca de San Diego, con lo que finaliza un viaje que llevó a los seres humanos al espacio sideral por primera vez desde 1972.
item.dc:creator: Kenneth Chang

media:content.media:credit: NASA, via Reuters

item.title: Eight Arrested in Connection With Deadly California Fireworks Explosion
item.description: The explosion last July in Esparto, Calif., killed seven people. Five of the eight arrested, including a former local police officer, have been charged with murder.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hippensteel

media:content.media:credit: Kent Porter/The Press Democrat, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Seven people died in the explosion at a fireworks warehouse in Esparto, Calif., last July.

item.title: California Warehouse Fire Suspect Invoked Luigi Mangione, Court Files Say
item.description: The suspect in a fire at a paper company facility in California complained about pay and raged against capitalism in videos posted to social media as he lit the blaze, prosecutors say.
item.dc:creator: Emma Schartz

media:content.media:credit: Qian Weizhong/VCG, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: The blaze caused more than $600 million in damage, officials said.

item.title: “It Was Survival Mode”: The Lunar Mission That Nearly Ended in Disaster
item.description: Fifty-six years ago, after a tense race to save the Apollo 13 crew, the astronauts finally splashed down safely. Here’s what flight directors who got them home remember.
item.dc:creator: Katherine Cusumano

media:content.media:credit: NASA

item.title: How Recovery Personnel Will Secure Artemis II Capsule at Sea After Splashdown
item.description: As the spacecraft bobs in the Pacific Ocean, recovery personnel from NASA and the U.S. Navy are out at sea, tracking the capsule and other pieces of jettisoned hardware.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Kenny Allen/NASA, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: U.S. Navy divers practiced procedures for recovering astronauts from a NASA space capsule after splashdown in 2023.

item.title: World Leaders Push to Save Iran Talks Amid Israel’s Attacks in Lebanon
item.description: President Trump’s cease-fire with Iran appears at risk as Vice President JD Vance heads to Pakistan for discussions with Iranian officials.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: David Guttenfelder/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A Lebanese man stood on a pile of debris in the Corniche El Mazraa area of Beirut after the Israeli bombardment of the capital on Wednesday.

item.title: Reseña de ‘Malcolm el de en medio’: nostalgia y risas
item.description: Dos décadas después, la serie recupera su esencia y demuestra que algunas dinámicas familiares —y frustraciones— nunca cambian.
item.dc:creator: James Poniewozik
item.category: Malcolm in the Middle: Life's Still Unfair (TV Program)

item.title: Here’s the Story of the Brady Bunch House
item.description: While only its exterior ever appeared in “The Brady Bunch,” stepping into the real-life Brady house now is like going back in time. Here’s a look.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Hubler and Gabriela Bhaskar

media:content.media:description: At the curb, a caramel-colored 1969 Plymouth Satellite station wagon of the type driven by Carol Brady is parked most days.

item.title: Banks Are Warned About Anthropic’s New, Powerful A.I. Technology
item.description: In an unusual move, the Treasury secretary and the Federal Reserve chair gathered bank executives to caution about cyberthreats posed by artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Rob Copeland and Colby Smith

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The stark message was delivered by Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent in a hastily called meeting.

item.title: What the Cease-Fire Means for Iran
item.description: Emerging from weeks of U.S. and Israeli airstrikes, an emboldened Iran has 10 demands for talks during the tenuous cease-fire, according to Iranian state media. Our reporter Erika Solomon assesses Iran’s position.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon, Christina Thornell, David Seekamp and Joey Sendaydiego

item.title: F.A.A. Says Military Can Use Anti-Drone Lasers in U.S. Airspace
item.description: An interagency dispute over the use of high-energy lasers near airports led the Federal Aviation Administration to temporarily close the airspace twice in Texas this year.
item.dc:creator: Karoun Demirjian
item.category: Laser (Light Amplification by Stimulated Emission of Radiation)

media:content.media:credit: Paul Ratje for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Locust, an anti-drone laser system that has been used recently along the border with Mexico, last month in Albuquerque, N.M.

item.title: Iran Unable to Find Mines It Planted in Strait of Hormuz, U.S. Says
item.description: The lost mines have prevented Iran from quickly complying with President Trump’s demand to allow more ships to pass through the waterway.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Reuters
media:content.media:description: Cargo ships near the Strait of Hormuz, as seen last month from northern Ras al-Khaimah in the United Arab Emirates.

item.title: Harris Signals Potential Third Presidential Run In 2028
item.description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris signaled a potential third run for the presidency in 2028 at a gathering of the National Action Network on Friday. More than a half-dozen other potential Democratic candidates also appeared at the event.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Mitssunaga

media:content.media:credit: Angelina Katsanis/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Kamala Harris, former vice president and the 2024 Democratic presidential candidate, spoke at the National Action Network Convention in New York on Friday.

item.title: University of Ottawa Lockdown Lifted After a Man Is Arrested
item.description: The university locked down the campus for just under two hours on Friday after reports that a man was acting suspiciously nearby. Police would not say if he was armed.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen

media:content.media:credit: Ian Austen/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The University of Ottawa in Canada’s capital on Friday.

item.title: New Evidence Further Implicates U.S. Missiles in Strikes That Killed 21 Civilians in Iran
item.description: Additional images and video build on an earlier analysis, which the Pentagon has disputed, showing Precision Strike Missiles, or PrSMs, hit a sports hall and residential areas in the Iranian city of Lamerd.
item.dc:creator: Christiaan Triebert, John Ismay, Alexander Cardia, Leanne Abraham and Azmat Khan
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Watch Live: Artemis II Crew Returns to Earth
item.description: The Artemis II crew returns to Earth after a historic lunar flyby.

media:content.media:credit: NASA, via Reuters

item.title: Trump Moves Top Aide James Blair to Political Operation Ahead of Midterms
item.description: Mr. Blair will “lead the charge from the outside,” the president said on social media. The move comes as Republicans are bracing for a potentially difficult November election.
item.dc:creator: Theodore Schleifer

media:content.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: James Blair, a deputy chief of staff, is taking over President Trump’s outside political operation and its $300 million-plus budget, Mr. Trump announced on Friday.

item.title: Eric Swalwell Faces Sex Assault Accusation as He Runs for California Governor
item.description: Eric Swalwell, a Democratic congressman, denied the accounts published by CNN and The San Francisco Chronicle. Nancy Pelosi was among those who said he should withdraw from the California governor’s race.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Rosenhall and Madison Malone Kircher

media:content.media:credit: Mike Kai Chen for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Campaign officials and supporters are abandoning Representative Eric Swalwell.

item.title: Trump-Ally Orban Trails in Polls Ahead of Hungary’s Election
item.description: After 16 years in power, Prime Minister Viktor Orban party is trailing in most independent polls behind the opposition leader Peter Magyar as Sunday’s election draws nearer.
item.dc:creator: Meg Felling

media:content.media:credit: Akos Stiller for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Peter Magyar at a rally last month for his party, Tisza, in Keszthely, Hungary. He has promised to improve relations with the European Union.

item.title: Eric Adams, Citizen of the World, Becomes an Actual Citizen of Albania
item.description: Mr. Adams, the former mayor of New York City, has expressed fondness for Albania, visiting the country in his last weeks in office.
item.dc:creator: Emma Goldberg

media:content.media:credit: Angelina Katsanis for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mayor Eric Adams has long had a soft spot in his heart for a certain Balkan nation.

item.title: Trump Is Racing to Redefine ‘America First’ in a Time of War
item.description: Washington has been debating the thrust of Mr. Trump’s foreign policy for more than a decade, with the only consensus being that “America First” means whatever the president says it does.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump holding a “Make America Great Again” hat on his way to his golf club in Miami last year.

item.title: Artemis II Astronauts Are Set to Arrive Home
item.description: Also, Iran adds a new condition as JD Vance heads to peace talks. Here’s the latest at the end of Friday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:credit: NASA
media:content.media:description: Clockwise from left: Christina Koch, Jeremy Hansen, Reid Wiseman and Victor Glover, on Monday.

item.title: 5 New Activewear Brands to Know
item.description: Five cross-functional activewear brands to put on your radar.
item.dc:creator: Gage Daughdrill

item.title: Possible Democratic 2028 Candidates Are Already Fighting for Black Voters’ Support
item.description: Possible presidential candidates are already courting a crucial Democratic constituency.
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck

media:content.media:credit: Angelina Katsanis/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Political activists watch former Vice President Kamala Harris speak during the National Action Network convention in Manhattan on Friday.

item.title: Davey Lopes, Base-Stealing Maestro, Is Dead at 80
item.description: He was one of baseball’s most efficient thieves, and part of a storied infield quartet with the Los Angeles Dodgers, playing in four World Series.
item.dc:creator: Richard Sandomir

media:content.media:credit: Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Davey Lopes, playing second base for the Los Angeles Dodgers in 1977, after tagging out Bake McBride of the Philadelphia Phillies.

item.title: Thomas S. Langner, Who Linked Social Ills to Mental Illness, Dies at 102
item.description: He helped lead what became known as the Midtown Manhattan Study, which showed that mental impairment is highly correlated with low socioeconomic status.
item.dc:creator: Michael S. Rosenwald

item.title: Eliot Engel, a 16-Term Bronx Liberal in Congress, Dies at 79
item.description: After defeating a Democratic fixture in a 1988 primary, he rose to lead the House Foreign Affairs Committee, then was toppled himself in 2020, undone by an overheard comment.
item.dc:creator: Sam Roberts

media:content.media:credit: Dith Pran/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Representative Eliot Engel in his Bronx office in 1998. He was a reliable champion of liberal causes.

item.title: With U.S.-Iran Peace Talks Set to Begin, Prospects for Long-Term Deal Uncertain
item.description: With U.S. and Iranian leaders heading to Pakistan for negotiations amid a two-week cease-fire, the two sides must resolve major differences on Lebanon, the Strait of Hormuz and frozen Iranian assets.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati, Adam Rasgon, Euan Ward and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Pool photo by Jacquelyn Martin
media:content.media:description: Before his departure on Friday for talks in Pakistan, Vice President JD Vance said he believed the negotiations were “going to be positive,” but he also had a warning for Iran.

item.title: Highlights From NASA’s Artemis II Moon Mission Splashdown
item.description: The crew of three Americans and one Canadian are to return to Houston on Saturday after concluding a journey that sent humans around the moon for the first time since 1972.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Bill Ingalls/NASA, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
